name: Nigeria
overview: Nigeria's Data Protection Act, 2023 (NDPA) replaced the NDPR-era framework with a full statute and an independent Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC). The NDPC has been an active young regulator — issuing the General Application and Implementation Directive (GAID) 2025, registering data controllers of major importance, and pursuing headline enforcement against banks, fintechs and social platforms.

legal_instruments.title: Nigeria Data Protection Act, 2023
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law: lawful bases, sensitive-data rules, data subject rights, DPO requirement for controllers of major importance, 72-hour breach notification, cross-border transfer conditions, and penalties up to the higher of NGN 10 million or 2% of annual gross revenue for major controllers.

legal_instruments.title: General Application and Implementation Directive (GAID), 2025
legal_instruments.summary: Replaces the NDPR 2019 implementation framework: detailed compliance mechanics including registration of controllers of major importance, annual audit filings (CADP), DPO certification and fees, effective from September 2025.

legal_instruments.title: Nigeria Data Protection Regulation (NDPR), 2019
legal_instruments.summary: Pre-statute regulation that seeded Nigeria's compliance ecosystem (audit filings, DPCOs); superseded by the NDPA and the GAID 2025.

developments.title: GAID 2025 takes effect
developments.summary: The NDPC's General Application and Implementation Directive became operative, formalising registration tiers, audit-filing deadlines and DPO credentialing under the NDPA.

developments.title: NDPC enforcement drive continues
developments.summary: The Commission announced investigations and remediation orders across banking, fintech, gaming and social media sectors, including multi-million-naira penalties.

penalties: Controllers of major importance: up to the higher of NGN 10 million or 2% of annual gross revenue of the preceding year; others: higher of NGN 2 million or 2% of annual gross revenue.
cross_border: Transfers require adequacy of the recipient jurisdiction (NDPC-assessed) or appropriate safeguards (contracts, BCRs, codes), or consent/other derogations under the NDPA.
